fields.panel2_caption: GPT-5.5 also fails at 30-year-old math problem
fields.panel3_caption: GPT-5.6 solves it in 148 minutes using materials already in the prompt
fields.panel4_caption: the answer was in the prompt the whole time, like it's been since 1979

narrative_summary: The joke escalates from two years of AI failure to a 148-minute triumph, then lands on the tragicomic kicker that the key construction — max of affine functions — was already sitting in the prompt, mirroring how the math world had the pieces since Nemirovsky & Yudin and just needed the right contractor.
rationale: Candidate 4 earns its punchline by closing the loop explicitly — the answer was in the prompt 'like it's been since 1979,' which ties the AI hype story back to Nemirovsky & Yudin and reframes the whole 30-year gap as a retrieval problem, not a discovery. The expanding-brain template is a perfect fit for an escalation that deflates at the top rather than inflating, and the specificity (GPT-5.4, 5.5, 5.6, 148 minutes, 1979) grounds every beat in the actual story.
story_title: GPT-5.6 used a prompt to close a 30-year gap in convex optimization
place: inside a Terms of Service agreement
hat_pair: dragonscale helm with smoke puffing out of tiny nostril-holes
